_meta.description: Verified connection matrix for endometriosis signature explorer visualization
_meta.verification_status: AWAITING KAREN REVIEW — every edge must be verified before visualization
_meta.notes: Every edge below is extracted from the signature page. NO edges are inferred or invented.

condition.label: Endometriosis
condition.framework: Ecological disease driven by metal-dependent microbial communities

enriched.label: Escherichia coli
enriched.role_summary: Primary pathogen — ticks ALL enzyme and feature boxes; estrogen deconjugation ability
enriched.notes: Ticks every box in the signature. The hub organism.

enriched.label: Bacteroides fragilis
enriched.role_summary: Zn-dependent BFT toxin, iron piracy, beta-glucuronidase, strict anaerobe indicating hypoxia
enriched.notes: Strict anaerobe — its presence is an INDICATOR of hypoxic conditions as well as a contributor. BFT = Bacteroides fragilis toxin, a zinc-metalloprotease.

enriched.label: Streptococcus agalactiae
enriched.role_summary: Group B Strep — Zn/Ni critical cofactors, beta-glucuronidase positive, iron piracy
enriched.notes: Iron piracy mentioned in role but siderophores not explicitly listed in the enzyme table for GBS. Candida enhances its epithelial colonization.

enriched.label: Fusobacterium nucleatum
enriched.role_summary: Ni-dependent glyoxalase (immune evasion) + hydrogenase (hypoxia) + O₂ consumption; NOT involved in estrogen deconjugation
enriched.notes: EXPLICITLY NOT involved in estrogen deconjugation. Glyoxalase and hydrogenase positive per Benoit/Maier — its immune evasion and hypoxia contributions RELY on nickel. Decreases local O₂ by metabolizing it. Co-aggregates with Candida show reduced cytokine output (functional shielding).
enriched.source_for_ni_enzymes: Benoit/Maier — verify in Maier 2019 (nickel in microbial pathogenesis) or re-ingest if F. nucleatum not explicitly listed

enriched.label: Candida albicans
enriched.role_summary: Functional shielding — biofilms consume O₂, co-aggregates shield bacteria from immune detection
enriched.notes: NOT noise. Critical interkingdom component that 16S studies miss. Nickel significantly increases co-aggregate biofilm biomass. Biofilms consume O₂ creating anaerobic pockets for B. fragilis and F. nucleatum. Co-aggregates with F. nucleatum show reduced cytokine output (functional shielding). Enhances epithelial colonization of Streptococcus.

enriched.label: Lactobacillus
enriched.role_summary: PARADOX: enriched in gut/peritoneum, DEPLETED in vaginal cavity — translocation likely
enriched.notes: Paradoxical finding. Gut microbiome is a better predictor of endometriosis than vaginal microbiome. Vaginal Lactobacillus depletion is well-documented (11.1% of papers). Glyoxalase enables persistence against neutrophils — even 'beneficial' organisms carry this nickel-dependent immune evasion tool.

depleted.label: Lachnospiraceae
depleted.why_lost: Lacked robust efflux pump systems to survive in iron-rich, pro-inflammatory environment

depleted.label: Ruminococcus
depleted.why_lost: Lacked defense systems for the metal-enriched niche

metals.role: Highest consensus — cofactor for urease, glyoxalase, hydrogenase; Candida biofilm enhancer; CONFIRMED metalloestrogen (Aquino 2012)
metals.metalloestrogen_mechanism: Binds ERα (noncompetitive — decreases estradiol binding sites without altering Kd); 2-5x cell growth increase at 10⁻⁹ to 10⁻⁶ M; induces cyclin D1 overexpression; epigenetic effects including histone deacetylation and H3K9 methylation (tumor suppressor silencing). 90.3% of endometriosis patients test positive for nickel allergic contact mucositis.

metals.role: Siderophore competition; functional anemia via hepcidin; retrograde menstruation exposure
Environmental burden; mis-metallation via Ca channels; synergistic with Cd for oxidative stress
metals.notes: Not a direct microbial cofactor — damages through mis-metallation. Synergistic with cadmium. Enters via calcium channels.

metals.role: Cofactor for superoxide dismutase in pathogenic taxa
metals.notes: Lower evidence weight. Included for completeness but not a primary driver in this signature.

metals.role: Mis-metallation; synergistic with Pb; CONFIRMED metalloestrogen (Aquino 2012)
metals.metalloestrogen_mechanism: Binds ERα with Kd ~4.5×10⁻¹⁰ M (near-equivalent to estradiol); activates GPR30/GPER at 50-500nM; activates ER target genes (cyclin D1, c-myc, cathepsin D). ALSO induces epigenetic changes, aneuploidy, and EMT independently of ER signaling.
metals.notes: Dual pathway to estrogen disruption: (1) direct ERα/GPR30 binding as metalloestrogen, (2) epigenetic silencing of tumor suppressors. Enters cells via calcium channels. Synergistic with lead for oxidative stress. Bioaccumulates in mammary tissue.

metals.role: Metalloprotease cofactor; calprotectin target; immune function

metals.notes: Minor role in this signature. Included for completeness.

enzymes.label: Zinc Metalloprotease
enzymes.function: Degrades host tissues, inhibits host immune system
enzymes.notes: In B. fragilis this is the BFT (Bacteroides fragilis toxin).

enzymes.label: NiFe-Hydrogenase (I, II, III)
enzymes.function: Decreases local O₂ saturation → hypoxic environment for strict/facultative anaerobes

enzymes.label: Nickel Urease
enzymes.function: Increases local pH via ammonia + CO₂ — disrupts vaginal Lactobacillus pH requirements
enzymes.notes: Listed taxa: 'E. coli, others' — only E. coli explicitly confirmed in this signature.

enzymes.label: Nickel Glyoxalase
enzymes.function: Enables persistence against neutrophils in blood — allows pathogens to circulate systemically
enzymes.notes: KEY INSIGHT: This is the immune evasion enzyme. Remove nickel, disable this across ALL glyoxalase-positive pathogens simultaneously — E. coli, F. nucleatum, AND Lactobacillus all lose immune evasion.

enzymes.label: Beta-glucuronidase
enzymes.function: Deconjugates estrogen glucuronides → increases hepatic estrogen recirculation (estrobolome)
enzymes.notes: No metal cofactor specified in signature data. This enzyme drives the estrobolome dysfunction.

enzymes.label: Siderophores
enzymes.function: Chelate and uptake host iron — essential for growth and biofilm formation
enzymes.notes: Iron acquisition mechanism. Lipocalin-2 specifically blocks siderophore-mediated iron uptake.

enzymes.label: O₂ Consumption Enzymes
enzymes.function: Metabolizes oxygen, decreasing local O₂ levels
enzymes.notes: Specific enzyme names not provided in signature. F. nucleatum's contribution to hypoxia is well-documented.

enzymes.label: Biofilm Formation / Functional Shielding
enzymes.function: Creates mixed biofilms that consume O₂, shield bacteria from immune detection, reduce host defenses
enzymes.notes: Candida-specific. Nickel significantly increases co-aggregate biofilm biomass. Co-aggregates with F. nucleatum show reduced cytokine output.

hallmarks.label: Hypoxia
hallmarks.description: Low oxygen environment driven by NiFe-hydrogenase (E. coli), O₂ consumption (F. nucleatum), and Candida biofilm O₂ depletion

hallmarks.label: Estrogen Recirculation
hallmarks.description: Beta-glucuronidase-positive taxa deconjugate estrogen glucuronides → hepatic estrogen recirculation → estrogen-dependent lesion growth
hallmarks.notes: Self-perpetuating: more estrogen → more lesion growth → more retrograde menstruation → more iron → more pathogen growth → more beta-glucuronidase → more estrogen

hallmarks.label: Biofilm Architecture
hallmarks.description: Siderophore-producing pathogens form iron-dependent biofilms, reinforced by Candida functional shielding

hallmarks.label: Functional Shielding
hallmarks.description: Candida-bacterial co-aggregates reduce cytokine output, shielding bacteria from neutrophils and NK cells

hallmarks.label: Fermentative Metabolism
hallmarks.description: Facultative anaerobes switch to fermentation in low O₂ → acidic, zinc-rich, iron-rich, pro-inflammatory microenvironment
hallmarks.notes: Self-perpetuating cycle: hypoxia → fermentation → growth-promoting environment → more pathogens → more hypoxia

hallmarks.label: pH Disruption
hallmarks.description: Nickel-dependent urease increases local pH via ammonia + CO₂, disrupting vaginal Lactobacillus pH requirements
hallmarks.notes: Also driven by fermentative metabolism making lesion microenvironment acidic

nutritional_immunity.label: Lactoferrin
nutritional_immunity.function: Chelates iron (and nickel) away from pathogens

nutritional_immunity.label: Hepcidin
nutritional_immunity.function: Withholds iron systemically — indicator of FUNCTIONAL ANEMIA, not true iron deficiency
nutritional_immunity.clinical_significance: CRITICAL: Low serum iron in endometriosis is a HOST DEFENSE STRATEGY. Iron supplementation is counterproductive.

nutritional_immunity.label: Lipocalin-2
nutritional_immunity.function: Siderophore-binding protein — blocks pathogen iron acquisition

nutritional_immunity.label: Glutathione
nutritional_immunity.function: ONLY factor that neutralizes cadmium and lead — its depletion leaves a critical gap in host defense
nutritional_immunity.clinical_significance: CRITICAL GAP: Without glutathione, cadmium and lead accumulate unchecked → mis-metallation → oxidative stress

dietary.label: Low-Nickel Diet
dietary.mechanism: Disables Ni-dependent glyoxalase (immune evasion), urease (pH disruption), hydrogenase (hypoxia); reduces Candida biofilm biomass

dietary.label: Low Red Meat Diet
dietary.mechanism: Reduces free iron and zinc available to pathogens

dietary.label: Low-Fat Diet
dietary.mechanism: Reduces E. coli growth (E. coli thrives on high-fat substrates)

dietary.label: Citrus Fruits
dietary.mechanism: Reduce circulating LPS; protective especially in smokers

dietary.label: Avoid Brassica Vegetables
dietary.mechanism: Brassicas are Cd/Pb/Zn/Ni hyperaccumulators — directly feed the metallomic signature

probiotic.label: E. coli Nissle 1917
probiotic.mechanism: Lacks virulence genes; outcompetes pathogenic E. coli, B. fragilis, GBS, F. nucleatum, C. albicans via superior siderophore systems + Ni uptake; competes in hypoxic environments

probiotic.label: Saccharomyces boulardii
probiotic.mechanism: Non-pathogenic yeast outcompetes Candida; cell walls bind cadmium and lead

supplemental.label: Lactoferrin Supplementation
supplemental.mechanism: Supports existing nutritional immunity (already elevated); chelates iron + nickel; ALTERNATIVE to iron supplementation

supplemental.label: NAC (N-Acetyl Cysteine)
supplemental.mechanism: Replenishes depleted glutathione — the only factor that neutralizes cadmium and lead

biophysical.label: Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy
biophysical.mechanism: Directly counters hypoxic niche exploited by strict/facultative anaerobes
biophysical.notes: Complete remission in animal model; not yet clinically investigated in humans

items.label: STOP: Iron Supplementation
items.conventional_rationale: Patient presents with anemia / low serum iron
items.why_counterproductive: Hepcidin elevation indicates FUNCTIONAL ANEMIA (host defense), NOT true deficiency. Iron feeds siderophore-producing pathogens, amplifies iron-rich pro-inflammatory environment.

items.label: STOP: Zinc Supplementation
items.conventional_rationale: Patient presents with low serum zinc
items.why_counterproductive: Calprotectin is already sequestering zinc as host defense. Zinc supplementation feeds Zn-metalloprotease-producing pathogens that degrade host tissues.

interkingdom_relationships.effect: Tight cohesion co-aggregates with reduced cytokine output — functional shielding from immune detection
Mixed biofilms that reduce host defenses; nickel increases biofilm biomass
Candida enhances epithelial colonization of Streptococcus

feedback_loops.label: Hypoxia Self-Perpetuation Cycle
steps: NiFe-hydrogenase + O₂ consumption + Candida biofilms deplete oxygen
Fermentative metabolism → acidic, iron-rich, zinc-rich environment
More pathogen biomass → more O₂ consumption

edge_list.mechanism: iron-rich environment overwhelms efflux pump capacity
lacked defense systems for metal-enriched niche
enters cells via calcium channels, displaces Zn/Fe
enters cells via calcium channels, displaces Zn/Fe
combined Cd+Pb produces synergistic oxidative stress and hypoxia
Cd binds ERα (Kd ~4.5×10⁻¹⁰ M) + activates GPR30 — direct estrogenic signaling independent of microbial beta-glucuronidase
Ni binds ERα (noncompetitive) — 2-5x cell growth; 90.3% of endo patients have Ni allergic contact mucositis; low-Ni diet improves gynecological symptoms

edge_list.effect: mixed biofilms reduce host defenses, Ni increases biomass
